input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS builds)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Version:** Install the latest stable release. MAUI docs list minimum supported versions at [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/).
- **Post-install:**
  ```bash
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version`

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest LTS or STS that supports MAUI.
- **Discover version:** The MAUI release notes list the minimum .NET SDK. Check [github.com/dotnet/maui/releases](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases).
- **Install:** Download the macOS (Arm64 or x64) installer.
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version`

## 3. .NET MAUI workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```
- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and platform dependencies.
- **Discover available versions:** `dotnet workload search maui`
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` (should show `maui`)

## 4. Android SDK & Java (Android builds)

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components automatically. If you need manual control:

- **Android Command-Line Tools:** [developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only)
- **Or** install Android Studio for the GUI manager: [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
- **Java:** MAUI bundles Microsoft's OpenJDK. To check: `dotnet android sdk info`
- **Set env vars** if needed:
  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
  export JAVA_HOME="/usr/lib/jvm/microsoft-17"  # path shown by workload
  ```
- **Verify:** `dotnet android sdk info` or `$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list`

## 5. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code + C# Dev Kit** | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) + `.NET MAUI` extension |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) (built-in MAUI support) |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** | Retired — use VS Code or Rider instead |

## 6. Create & run a test project

```bash
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# iOS (requires Xcode simulator or device)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios        # adjust TFM to your SDK version

# Android (starts emulator or uses connected device)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 7. Full environment health check

```bash
dotnet workload list
dotnet new maui --dry-run
xcodebuild -version
echo $ANDROID_HOME
```

For a one-command diagnostic, install the MAUI-check tool:
```bash
dotnet tool install -g Redth.Net.Maui.Check
maui-check
```
This audits every dependency and offers to fix issues automatically.

---

**Key rule of thumb:** Start from the [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) for your target version — they specify exact .NET SDK, Xcode, and Android SDK minimums. Everything else flows from there.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking for a comprehensive setup guide for .NET MAUI development on macOS targeting iOS and Android. This is a knowledge/advice question - I don't need to search a codebase or run commands. I should provide a clear, structured plan based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI development setup.

Let me provide a thorough but well-organized response.
